title: Authority

definition.short: The recognized right to direct, decide, or govern within a defined structure.
definition.full: Authority refers to a recognized and accepted right to make decisions, set direction, or govern within a social, legal, institutional, or relational context.

coreMeaning: Legitimate decision-making power within a recognized context.

sourceReferences.label: Oxford Languages
sourceReferences.notes: Reference input only. Runtime output should use internal wording.

sourceReferences.label: John Rawls, A Theory of Justice

perspectives.summary: Authority should remain publicly justifiable through fairness.
